title: Zero-config dev server mode with sensible defaults
purpose: A developer should be able to run 'cargo run -p aion-server' with zero flags and zero config files and get a working dev server. Temporal's 'temporal server start-dev' is the bar. Currently the server requires a TOML config file and manually editing workflow_packages. This brief makes the no-config path work with sensible dev defaults.
task: Make aion-server start with sensible defaults when no config file is found: in-memory store, localhost:50051 gRPC + localhost:8080 HTTP, default namespace, no auth, metrics enabled. Auto-discover .aion package files in the current directory. Print a clear startup banner showing connection info and loaded packages. Add a --workflow-package CLI flag so packages can be loaded without editing config files.

requirements.title: Server starts with no config file
requirements.spec: WHEN aion-server is started with no --config flag and no aion.toml in the working directory, THE SYSTEM SHALL start with: in-memory event store, gRPC on 127.0.0.1:50051, HTTP on 127.0.0.1:8080, namespace mode SharedEngine, default namespace 'default', auth disabled, metrics enabled. THE SYSTEM SHALL log a clear startup banner showing the listening addresses.
acceptance: 'cargo run -p aion-server' with no config file starts without error.
Startup log shows gRPC and HTTP addresses.
The CLI tool can connect and run 'aion list' against the default server.

requirements.title: Auto-discover workflow packages and --workflow-package flag
requirements.spec: WHEN aion-server starts, THE SYSTEM SHALL scan the current directory for files matching *.aion and load them as workflow packages. THE SYSTEM SHALL accept --workflow-package PATH flags to load additional packages. Auto-discovered and CLI-specified packages SHALL be merged with any packages listed in the config file.
acceptance: Placing hello-world.aion in the working directory and running aion-server loads it automatically.
'cargo run -p aion-server -- --workflow-package examples/hello-world/hello-world.aion' loads the package.
Startup log shows each loaded package name.
Config file workflow_packages and CLI --workflow-package are additive.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the config file format — only add defaults when no config is provided.
SHALL NOT remove support for explicit config files — they remain the production path.
SHALL NOT add database auto-creation or migration — in-memory is the dev default.
verification: 'cargo run -p aion-server' with no config starts and accepts CLI connections.
'cargo run -p aion-server -- --workflow-package examples/hello-world/hello-world.aion' loads the package.
